## [00:00 - 03:03]

So here's a super simple video probably the simplest video in the whole training Which is how to add the UTM tracking to your ad? So what you do you come to this page which is the Facebook ads attribution tracking trinity campaign breakdown Copy this line here. I've conveniently left it for you. So you can literally just copy that whole line Go into ads manager Find the ad or the ads that you want to add it to so let's just say We wanted to add it to this ad here, and let's say it didn't already have some tracking You go down into URL Parameters, this is really annoying the way Facebook loads this and you paste it here and we go Oops, there we go delete that and we will paste it there That is it. So what this does is this gets attached to your link in the ad on The button on the link etc. So that when someone clicks your ad that gets pushed into your website Assuming you have Google Analytics installed that data will go into Google Analytics This UTM campaign equals campaign name these curly brackets tell Facebook That the thing inside is a variable all that says is replace this thing inside with something that Facebook recognizes So campaign dot name translates to the text in your actual Campaign and this is why it's really important if you want to get the best out of this Use campaign names and ad set names and ad names that are going to make sense to you in Google Analytics So if you have a top or funnel mid bottom or cold warm hot if you're targeting certain countries if you're doing certain placements Or you have certain creatives or themes or angles put those in your ad Name in your ad set name or your campaign name as needed so that data gets passed to Facebook automatically FYI as far as Facebook is concerned on this once this is set if you change the name of the campaign and You know you're pushing that data to Google Analytics and you change it a week later a month later You're gonna screw up your data now one thing that Facebook have said recently is that will no longer happen So once you set this and you launch that ad into you know you publish the ad that Campaign name sticks with the ad so even if you change the name of the campaign It will still show the same campaign name here because it helps your tracking But don't rely on that so just be mindful of that that if you are you know you've called your ad your campaign top of funnel Purchase Purchases worldwide or something and then you change the name After you've started to send that data to Google Analytics to something else It can also be changed in Google Analytics and what Google will see is oh look there's a new campaign So when you look at your campaign data, it's no longer linked to the original campaign that the ad was launched with

## [03:03 - 03:13]

Just bear that in mind when you're making changes and things with the campaigns as it's an ads It can impact the data that you'll see in in Google Analytics as well
